comments.author: @luiscolt8208
comments.text: The profound hospitality of the Paraguayan people is a direct response to their history of survival. Following devastating conflicts such as the War of the Triple Alliance and the Chaco War, the country was left demographically and economically decimated. Faced with the massive loss of men, national reconstruction and survival depended fundamentally on community solidarity, mutual support, and *jopói* (lending a hand or reciprocal aid). Historically, isolation and a lack of resources compelled families to share the little they had with others. This resilience became a cultural pillar; hospitality toward visitors, foreigners, and neighbors solidified into both a survival mechanism and an act of affirming life in the face of tragedy.
